About the Programme

Transforming NTD Data Capacity
Through Generative AI

AMPLIFY is a pioneering professional development programme jointly delivered by WHO's ESPEN and Sightsavers. It equips Ministry of Health staff leading national Neglected Tropical Disease (NTD) programmes with practical skills in generative AI — enabling better data analysis, faster reporting, and more impactful training materials.

🎯 The Problem

National NTD programmes have limited access to capacity-building in data analytics. This restricts data use beyond basic reporting and impedes the feedback loop between data quality and programmatic decision-making. Existing training materials are often static and slow to evolve.

💡 The Solution

AMPLIFY is built entirely on free AI tools and services — deliberately chosen so that the skills participants build are not dependent on ongoing donor funding or paid subscriptions. The focus is on sustainable, self-sufficient capacity that countries can continue independently.

📊 How It Works

Participants learn to use freely available AI tools through hands-on training sessions and monthly check-ins with expert instructors. Assignments are grounded in real NTD datasets, ensuring skills transfer directly to participants' day-to-day programme work.

🌍 Why It Matters

By embedding AI literacy within national NTD teams, AMPLIFY creates a lasting foundation for data-driven decision-making — improving programme quality, technical working group effectiveness, and the reach of health worker training materials.

2026 Pilot Cohort

16 Professionals. 7 Countries.
One Mission.

Our inaugural cohort brings together Ministry of Health NTD professionals from across sub-Saharan Africa — each nominated by their country to serve as an AI champion within their national programme.

Course Schedule

Seven Sessions, March–September 2026

AMPLIFY runs monthly from March through September 2026, building progressively from AI fundamentals through to autonomous AI workflows and programme integration.

AMPLIFY 2026 · Seven-module programme · March 19 — September 17

Programme Objectives

What AMPLIFY Sets Out to Achieve

AMPLIFY is designed to demonstrate the transformative potential of generative AI in data analysis and training material development — leading to more informed decision-making and improved programme outcomes across participating countries.

🛠️

Free Tools Only

Built on freely available AI tools so skills endure beyond any single project or funding cycle

📚

Training

Comprehensive training covering data analysis, visualisation, and content creation

🔬

Applied Practice

Real-world application in technical working groups such as national NOEC meetings

🔄

Ongoing Support

Monthly virtual check-ins to address challenges and share best practices

📈

Scalability

Pilot designed to lay groundwork for broader adoption across more countries

🌐

Impact

Better data quality, stronger programme decisions, improved training materials

Delivered In Partnership

A Joint WHO ESPEN & Sightsavers Initiative

AMPLIFY is co-designed and co-delivered by two leading organisations in global NTD elimination — combining WHO's ESPEN technical expertise with Sightsavers' commitment to building AI capability within national health systems.
